dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9515 IH: Marketplace Fraud Accountability Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Grothman (for himself, Mr. Fine, and Mr. Arrington) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to require the use of multi-factor authentication to access certain information through healthcare.gov.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Marketplace Fraud Accountability Act or the MFA Act.

section.header: Requiring the use of multi-factor authentication to access certain information through healthcare.gov

subsection.text: Section 1311(c) of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (42 U.S.C. 18031(c)) is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

subparagraph.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), not later than the date that is 1 year after the date of enactment of this paragraph, the Secretary shall require the use of multi-factor authentication to verify the identity of any individual attempting to access the healthcare.gov website (or any successor website) for purposes of—

clause.text: enrolling in a qualified health plan offered through an Exchange; or
accessing any personalized information with respect to such enrollment.

subparagraph.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply in the case of an individual that does not have access to broadband service or cellular service, or is otherwise unable to use multi-factor authentication for reasons specified by the Secretary.
For purposes of this paragraph, the term multi-factor authentication means authentication through the verification of 2 or more of the following types of authentication factors:

clause.text: Knowledge factors, such as a password.
Possession factors, such as a token.
Inherence factors, such as biometric characteristics.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this subsection shall apply with respect to plan years beginning on or after the date that is 1 year after the date of enactment of this subsection.
